steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim requires a quotation that uses natural imagery in a comparison to convey Demetrio's mental state. Two requirements: (1) an explicit comparison using a natural image, and (2) the comparison describes his thoughts or mind.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "The river crept along, murmuring as the waters rose and fell in small cascades.": describes a physical scene in nature but contains no comparison to Demetrio's mental state. ✗
  • Choice B — "Demetrio suppressed a sigh. Memories crowded and buzzed through his brain like bees about a hive.": the simile 'like bees about a hive' is an explicit natural comparison applied directly to his mental state — chaotic, swarming thoughts. ✓
  • Choice C — "When he reached the summit, he glanced down to see the sun steeping the valley in a lake of gold.": uses natural imagery but describes an external scene, not a comparison to his mind. ✗
  • Choice D — "Demetrio laid a hand on his hair which covered his ears, and stretching himself out for a longtime, seemed to be lost in thought.": presents a sensory observation without comparing it to his mental state. ✗
B is correct.
